title: epiphyllum crenatum subsp. kimnachii
intro: Epiphyllum crenatum subsp. kimnachii, also known as the Mexican climbing cactus, is a rare epiphytic cactus native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its waxy, white flowers and cylindrical stems. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Epiphyllum crenatum subsp. kimnachii include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing its stems in hot water, creating a tea that can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
